rulings.Case Title: DAMIEN QUINN VS. BELLROSE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Strike Second Amended Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 15, 2019 line 1. PLAINTIFF DAMIEN QUINN Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Strike Second Amended Cross Complaint DENIED. An attack on an entire cause of action is grounds for demurrer, not a motion to strike. See Quiroz v. Seventh Ave. Center (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 1256, 1281. Additionally, May 1, 2019 ruling regarding Judgment on the Pleadings in vacated as moot. Parties should have, but failed to inform the Court that an amended operative complaint had been submitted for e-filing (but not yet accepted) prior to May 1, 2019 hearing. Judgment on the Pleadings was moot prior to the issuance of the tentative ruling and hearing. Further, while cause of action for declaratory relief in a lengthy narrative appears to be duplicative of the relief available under the Quiet Title cause of action asserted in this case, some portions of the relief sought are not necessarily (as alleged) related solely to the title, e.g. paragraph 70. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DAMIEN QUINN VS. BELLROSE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Demurrer To Second Amended Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 15, 2019 line 1. PLAINTIFF DAMIEN QUINN Notice Of Hearing On Demurrer To Second Amended Cross Complaint SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to all causes of action addressed in the demurrer. While Cross-Complainant filed an opposition to the demurrer, opposition failed to address each argument raised as to each cause of action. Opposition selectively argued re: particularity and propriety of abuse of process, leaving out other issues raised, e.g. RICO elements, Wilhelm v. Prey, Price, Qilliam & Russel (1986) 186 Cal.App.3d 1324, stipulation to consolidate, etc. = (501/REQ)
